text.title: Insight to Design Ticket Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that govern the conversion of research insights to design-backlog tickets. These prevent the most common failure: insights that never become work.

rule.statement: Each ticket carries exactly ONE insight. Reject tickets that bundle 2+ insights, even if related. Bundling makes prioritization impossible and obscures evidence.
rule.rationale: Bundled tickets become "boil the ocean" requests no designer pulls. One-insight-per-ticket creates tractable units the designer can finish in 1-3 days.

rule.statement: Every ticket includes ≥ 2 evidence references: transcript_id + timestamp OR journey-map node + observation_id. Reject tickets relying on "user research said" without specific citations.
rule.rationale: Evidence-free tickets get pushed back ("show me where you saw this") and waste discovery cycles. Citations resolve this in seconds.

rule.statement: The ticket's "direction" field is a suggestion, not a mandate. Designer may diverge from the suggestion in their solution. Reject tickets containing prescriptive solutions ("add a modal with two buttons") instead of problem statements.
rule.rationale: Prescriptive tickets reduce designer space and produce inferior outputs. The insight defines WHAT users struggle with; the designer chooses HOW.

rule.statement: Each ticket receives an ICE score (Impact / Confidence / Ease 1-10) with documented rationale BEFORE entering the active backlog. PM and researcher must both sign. Reject queueing without ICE score.
rule.rationale: Backlogs without scoring become FIFO queues. ICE forces explicit cost/benefit and surfaces the highest-leverage tickets.

rule.statement: Tickets that sit un-pulled for > 90 days are tagged "retire_candidate" and reviewed at the next backlog grooming. Either re-validate with new evidence OR retire. Reject indefinitely-aging tickets — they pollute prioritization signal.
rule.rationale: Stale tickets create the illusion of a healthy backlog while diluting attention. Forced retirement keeps the backlog meaningful.

example.title: One-insight ticket with evidence
example.code: TICKET T-0142 title: Checkout step 3 produces last-second hesitation insight: Users pause 8-22 seconds at step 3 (shipping cost reveal), 4 of 6 interviewees re-checked the total before continuing. evidence: transcript_a3f-0042 @ 00:14:18, journey-map node "Checkout-S3" direction (suggested, not mandated): explore upfront shipping-cost disclosure OR step-3 confidence signal ICE: I=8 C=6 E=5 → 6.3 researcher_sign: yes (jdoe) pm_sign: yes (asmith) acceptance: designer produces ≥ 1 candidate solution + posts back to research for evidence-fit check
example.note: Illustrates r1-r4: one insight, two evidence references, suggested direction, ICE scored.

example.title: Solution disguised as insight
example.code: TICKET T-0143 title: Add progress indicator to checkout description: Users feel lost; let's add a 3-step progress bar at the top of every checkout page. Use Tailwind, with a green checkmark for completed steps. evidence: (none)
example.note: Illustrates r2 + r3 violations. No evidence, solution prescribed before designer has space to diverge.
